examples.title: Simple Access Check Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates basic permission checking mechanism similar to UNIX file permissions
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CheckAccess(user, file, requestedOperation)
BEGIN
  IF user.id == file.owner.id THEN
    RETURN CheckPermission(file.ownerPermissions, requestedOperation)
  ELSE IF user.group == file.group THEN
    RETURN CheckPermission(file.groupPermissions, requestedOperation)
  ELSE
    RETURN CheckPermission(file.otherPermissions, requestedOperation)
  ENDIF
END

FUNCTION CheckPermission(permissions, operation)
BEGIN
  SWITCH operation OF
    CASE READ: RETURN permissions.read
    CASE WRITE: RETURN permissions.write
    CASE EXECUTE: RETURN permissions.execute
  ENDSWITCH
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm mimics UNIX file permission checking. It first checks if the user is the owner, then checks group membership, and finally falls back to 'others' permissions.

examples.title: Role-Based Access Control Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates RBAC with users, roles, and permissions
examples.explanation: This Python implementation shows how RBAC works: users are assigned roles, roles have permissions, and access is checked by verifying if user's roles have the required permission.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of roles per user
examples.spaceComplexity: O(u + r) where u = users, r = roles

examples.title: Capability-Based Access Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates capability list concept where each process carries its access rights
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates capability-based access control where each process (subject) carries its own list of capabilities (tokens) that specify what resources it can access and what operations are permitted.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of capabilities
examples.spaceComplexity: O(c) where c = MAX_CAPABILITIES per process

examples.title: Authentication Check Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AuthenticateUser(username, password, biometric_data, token_code)
BEGIN
  // Step 1: Verify password
  IF NOT VerifyPassword(username, password) THEN
    RETURN AUTH_FAILED
  ENDIF
  
  // Step 2: If two-factor enabled, verify token
  IF user.two_factor_enabled THEN
    IF NOT VerifyToken(username, token_code) THEN
      RETURN AUTH_FAILED
    ENDIF
  ENDIF
  
  // Step 3: If biometric required, verify biometric
  IF user.biometric_required THEN
    IF NOT VerifyBiometric(username, biometric_data) THEN
      RETURN AUTH_FAILED
    ENDIF
  ENDIF
  
  // Step 4: Generate session token
  session_token = GenerateSessionToken(username)
  RETURN AUTH_SUCCESS + session_token
END

FUNCTION GenerateSessionToken(username)
BEGIN
  timestamp = GetCurrentTime()
  random_value = GenerateRandom(64)
  token = Hash(username + timestamp + random_value)
  RETURN token
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates multi-factor authentication combining knowledge factor (password), possession factor (token), and inherence factor (biometric). Only after all factors are verified is a session token generated.
